reviews.review_msg: Recommend

His lectures might be boring to some of the students. Because his voice is not high nor enthusiasm. But he will write his notes while he is speaking. And that is the most important key point for this corse!!!! It helps you know what is the steps when he solving the problems, what is he talking and how is he thinking, and how you should learn MATH.

He is an excellent teacher: clear, organized, informative. And there is something charming in his attitude: he is not really snob, but he knows to keep a distance - he is hot and cold at the same time. He makes me feel like it was in my childhood, when I tried to be a good boy. I know that other students feel similar. Nice experience.
Very nice prof. Definitely he loves teaching. Classes are not tedious. In fact, I spend quite lot of time on assignments. Fair marking. Midterms are passable.
Likerecommend this prof. midterms follow both assignment questions and class notes. marking is very fair. classes are well structured. he gives clear examples to the course is not stuffy.
Literally, one of the best professors at SFU. Very clear on what he talks about, well organized notes. I agree with prev. posts, he seems to love lecturing. Straightforward exams, fair marking.

Overall, he is a good prof. He is maybe not talented in teaching but really passionate and makes lots of effort into the course for students. Lectures are more theoretical, while tutorials are for problem solving. Assignments are very time consuming and expect you to use a Math software like Maple. Exams are fair where the class avg is about 65%.
Highly recommend. He knows how to teach and he makes it interesting. Be careful, he asks quit lot of 'must understand' questions, definitions, theorems, etc...

really nice man. He is not the kind of skilled math teacher, but he will answer you question in patience. make few mistake in class but not a big deal, very clear class note, and very nice on your mark. Most importantly, very easy exam, almost everything you have done from notes and assignment.
This guy is awesome
Best professor ever. Learned more in this course than I learned in my whole life prior and since.

Love this prof. He is so gentle, so courteous. He knows exactly what to teach, when and how: no panic, no rash. Though, notes are not trivial, quit lot of theory. Room 108 seats, enrollment 103. Only few free seats each time. So, take your own conclusions. The first midterm was quite successful; still have the second one and the final to reap.

His test were very well written. Good evaluations. He appears to love being a prof, there is euphoria behind his eyes when he goes up to teach. It's kinda great. He can be unclear if you zone out for a bit, then come back to it (lullaby voice). I found him to be my best professor of the semester (not a great selection though).
like this prof
Course is not easy. This prof does well his job. Do not miss his classes, the midterms/exam are strongly related.
good prof

He is very unorganized. Always cannot finish the material before the test and assignment, so no time to review the staff. The course is very boring. The test is not reasonable and shouldn't be tested on paper since we learned to calculate the huge number by computer for a whole term.
Perfectly organized (but) challenging course. Interesting.

Very understanding professor. Genuinely wants his students to pass. Exams are pretty straightforward. MUST ATTEND THE TUTORIAL. Lectures are pure theory can can get quite boring. Tutorials are set up to help the HW assignments. Overall a great professor.
a very nice prof, dedicated and tries hard to explain. midterms / exams are not so easy, take care. I got my A-, but it wasn't simple....
I really enjoyed the class, well organized. He keeps it interesting and the text is easier to understand after while. Definitely, I recommend to take him.

You can tell it is his first time teaching at SFU.There were several format changes of the tests over the semester which made it somewhat difficult to prepare for.  That being said, he made tons of time available for office hours and tried his very best to explain things during that period.His lectures were theoretical so the tutorial was necessary
Not a great prof, not a great teacher - not horrible and seems like a nice guy, but not great, really hard to understand and really easy to skip !
